package handler

import (
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "errors"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "strings"
)

// Positive scenario for handler_switches_on_error_strings: risky concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: Handlers that branch on `err.Error()` text rather than typed errors.
type CaseHandlerSwitchesOnErrorStringsDTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseHandlerSwitchesOnErrorStringsModel struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseHandlerSwitchesOnErrorStringsRepository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseHandlerSwitchesOnErrorStringsModel
}

type CaseHandlerSwitchesOnErrorStringsService struct {
    repo *CaseHandlerSwitchesOnErrorStringsRepository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CaseHandlerSwitchesOnErrorStringsAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func PositiveHandlerSwitchesOnErrorStrings(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseHandlerSwitchesOnErrorStringsDTO) error {
    err := errors.New("record not found")
    switch err.Error() {
    case "record not found":
        c.JSON(http.StatusNotFound, gin.H{"error": "not found"})
    case "duplicate key":
        c.JSON(http.StatusConflict, gin.H{"error": "conflict"})
    default:
        c.JSON(http.StatusInternalServerError, gin.H{"error": "error"})
    }
    return nil
}
